text: Working with APIs allows applications to communicate and share data over the internet. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you usually receive a response in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read and process. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to verify who is making the request. It is important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, good error handling is essential—you should check the status code of each response and handle errors like “404 Not Found” or “429 Too Many Requests” gracefully.
